1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
7.     <PropertyGroup>
8.       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
9.     </PropertyGroup>
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
11.   </Target>
12. 
13.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
14.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
15.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>
25.

data.content:
## Incremental Build Issues Found

Both custom targets break incremental builds:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11)
**Problem:** Writes `DateTime.Now` to `build-timestamp.txt` on every build. The file content always changes, which invalidates downstream caching (e.g., `CoreCompile` sees a modified input) and forces recompilation even when source code hasn't changed.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23)
**Problem:** Unconditionally runs `git rev-parse HEAD` and overwrites `git-hash.g.cs` every build. Even when the git hash hasn't changed, the file is rewritten (new timestamp on disk), causing MSBuild to treat it as modified and re-trigger compilation.

**Neither target has `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes**, so MSBuild has no way to skip them — they always execute.

---

### Fixes

| Target | Fix |
|--------|-----|
| `GenerateTimestamp` | Remove it, or add `Inputs`/`Outputs` and only write when a source file is newer than the timestamp file. |
| `EmbedGitHash` | Add `Inputs="$(MSBuildAllProjects)"` / `Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"`, and use a temp file + diff so the `.g.cs` is only overwritten when the hash actually changes. |

Would you like me to fix these targets?
